# ReportAfrica

> Africa's Citizen-Powered Live Reporting Platform

ReportAfrica is a real-time citizen journalism and community reporting platform built for Africa. Ordinary citizens become first reporters on the ground — reporting traffic, emergencies, corruption, elections, and humanitarian crises instantly from their phones.

## What ReportAfrica Does
- Citizen incident reporting with photo, video, and live streaming
- AI-powered content moderation and fake news verification
- Real-time geo-based incident map across 32+ African countries
- Election monitoring with parallel vote tabulation
- Community Helping Hands — verified humanitarian fundraising campaigns
- Media licensing marketplace for citizen-captured content
- Government and NGO civic intelligence dashboards
- Trust scoring system that rewards credible reporters
- Anonymous reporting with identity protection and face blur
- Emergency SOS with automatic location sharing

## Who It's For
- Citizens across Africa who want to report and stay informed
- Journalists and media houses seeking verified citizen content
- NGOs and humanitarian organizations monitoring crises
- Government agencies tracking civic incidents
- Election observers and monitoring bodies

## Coverage
32+ African countries including Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, Uganda, Rwanda, Tanzania, Ethiopia, Senegal, Cameroon, Egypt, Morocco, and more.

## Key URLs
- Platform: https://www.reportafrica.africa
- Feed: https://www.reportafrica.africa/feed
- Elections: https://www.reportafrica.africa/elections
- Donations: https://www.reportafrica.africa/donations
- Insights: https://www.reportafrica.africa/insights
- Business: https://www.reportafrica.africa/business
- Government: https://www.reportafrica.africa/government

## Tech
Mobile (React Native/Expo), Web (Next.js), Backend (NestJS), PostgreSQL + PostGIS, AWS S3, AWS IVS livestreaming, Mapbox, Paystack payments, Firebase notifications.
